baseline.timestamp_interpretation: vendor one-minute labels are bar-close; subtract one minute before resampling

scope_locks: No change to entry, BOS, sweep, stop, target, cost, or exit parameters.
All higher-timeframe features use only completed bars available before range_start.
Weekday and session cells are attribution diagnostics, not selectable filters.
No interaction tests until the corresponding univariate families are complete.
Historical results cannot authorize deployment because the timing baseline was selected in-sample.

observation_units.primary: eligible session opportunity, including zero return when no retained trade is taken

families.hypothesis: HTF directional context changes reversal quality; no direction is assumed superior ex ante.
The immediately preceding completed 4H trend state contains incremental information.
Agreement between independent completed HTFs is more informative than either alone.

features: previous completed ETH-day ATR20 percentile over trailing 126 days
families.hypothesis: The reversal edge is regime-dependent and may degrade in extreme volatility.
The existing 5-minute nontrend filter may require confirmation from a higher timeframe.

features: initial range divided by prior D1 ATR20
families.hypothesis: Very small ranges create noise sweeps while very large ranges impair reward-to-risk.

features: ATR20 versus five completed days earlier
RV20 versus five completed days earlier
families.hypothesis: A transition into expansion differs from a mature high-volatility regime.

features: directional distance from range edge to prior-day high or low
range midpoint location inside prior-day range
families.hypothesis: Sweeps near established daily liquidity levels are more meaningful than isolated intraday sweeps.

features: directional distance to previous completed week high or low
position relative to previous week midpoint
families.hypothesis: Weekly extremes provide higher-timeframe liquidity context.

features: range opening price minus previous ETH-day close, normalized by ATR
gap direction relative to trade direction
families.hypothesis: A reversal that closes an overnight displacement differs from one extending it.

features: initial-range volume relative to trailing same-session median
families.hypothesis: Participation may separate meaningful liquidity events from thin-market noise.

features: range midpoint relative to completed ETH VWAP at range end
families.hypothesis: The DTR reversal may work best when it returns toward established session value.

requirements: Both parent families show stable directional effects in at least four of six half-years.
Each parent retains at least 55 percent of baseline trades or 250 trades, whichever is lower.
Interaction definition is fixed before its result is inspected.

promotion_requirements: Retain at least 250 trades and at least 50 percent of baseline eligible sessions.
Improve expectancy by at least 0.03R or reduce max drawdown by at least 20 percent without reducing expectancy.
Improve return/DD by at least 15 percent.
Positive net R in 2023, 2024, and 2025, or two positive years with the third no worse than -5R.
Positive aggregate expectancy under two-tick slippage each side.
No single session or half-year contributes more than 60 percent of retained net R.
Survive family-level joint resampling; otherwise remain diagnostic only.
